zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個外送來了還要先靜音五秒、確定對方按對門才敢開門的朋友——我們不是怕生，是怕社交
variations: 成年人最害怕的兩個字：門鈴。

en.editorNote: Send to the friend who mutes their TV when the delivery guy arrives just in case — we're not antisocial, we're door-phobic

Junior adult and unknown numbers: ignore on principle. Senior adult: just answer, it's faster than worrying about it.

items.sourceNote: fitted sheet folding adulting meme universal; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
小時候以為長大就什麼都會做，
長大才發現有些事學不會就是學不會。

例如：摺床包。

我可以做季度報表、可以跟客戶談合約、可以一個人搬家，
但我永遠不會摺床包。

床包進到我手裡，最後都會變成一團——
大團，但是是團。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個衣櫃裡有一團「應該是床包」的東西的朋友——這不是失敗，這是時代的共同遺憾
variations: 成年人最大的謊言是：「我之後會摺好。」
床包就像感情，越想把它弄整齊，越亂。

en.content:
As a kid you thought adults could do everything.
As an adult you learn there are things you will never master.

Example: folding a fitted sheet.

I can run quarterly reports, negotiate contracts, move apartments solo —
but a fitted sheet in my hands will always end up as a ball.

A neat ball. But still a ball.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose linen closet has one shape: 'lump' — this isn't failure, it's a shared generational defeat
variations: An adult's biggest lie: 'I'll fold it properly later.'
Fitted sheets are like relationships. The harder you try to smooth them out, the messier they get.

items.sourceNote: fridge mystery container forgotten food adulting; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人的冰箱深處是一個平行宇宙。

那盒不知道什麼時候放進去的便當。
那罐三個月前打開過一次的醬料。
那包永遠拿不出來的青菜。

我每次打開冰箱都跟它對視五秒，然後關上。

我們都知道對方在那裡。
我們都假裝對方不在那裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個冰箱深處藏著「文物」的朋友——丟掉它需要勇氣，那是一種告別
variations: 成年人最有勇氣的時刻：清冰箱。
冰箱裡的東西分兩種：要吃的，跟「等一下再說」的。

en.content:
The back of an adult's fridge is a parallel universe.

That takeout container from an unknown date.
That sauce I opened once, three months ago.
That bag of greens that will never come out.

Every time I open the fridge we make eye contact for five seconds, then I close it.

We both know it's in there.
We both pretend it isn't.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose fridge has artifacts in the back — throwing it out takes real courage, it's basically a goodbye
variations: An adult's bravest act: cleaning out the fridge.
Fridge food comes in two categories: food, and 'I'll deal with it later.'

items.sourceNote: random embarrassing memory intrusive thought adulting meme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人大腦的內建功能：

你正在好好走路、好好洗澡、好好排隊買咖啡，
突然——

國中那次喊老師「媽」。
大學那次傳訊息傳錯人。
上禮拜開會講錯數字還沒人糾正你。

大腦：「該複習一下囉。」
我：「我沒有要——」
大腦：「來，從最尷尬那一段開始。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個會對著空氣突然「嘖」一聲的朋友——你不是怪，你是大腦在自動播放黑歷史
variations: 成年人的詛咒：在最平靜的時候，想起最丟臉的事。
我以為時間會沖淡一切，沒想到時間只是把它存檔，三年後再寄回來給我。

en.content:
An adult brain's built-in feature:

You're walking normally, showering normally, standing in line for coffee normally,
and then —

that time you called the teacher 'mom' in middle school.
that text you sent to the wrong person in college.
that number you said wrong in last week's meeting that nobody corrected.

Brain: 'Time for your daily replay.'
Me: 'I didn't ask for —'
Brain: 'Starting with the most cringe one. Enjoy.'
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who randomly says 'ugh' at nothing in public — you're not weird, your brain is just auto-playing your greatest hits
variations: The adult curse: remembering your worst moment during your most peaceful one.
I thought time healed all wounds. Turns out time just files them, then mails them back to you three years later.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人/高級大人 電鍋水量 過年返家 Threads 2026 viral; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人煮飯：站在電鍋前面，盯著米發呆，最後 google「兩杯米要加多少水」。

高級大人煮飯：眼睛掃一下，手指插進米裡比一下，水量就對了。

我月薪八萬、會做財報、能管十個專案，但只要回家走進廚房，我就會變回那個問媽媽「鹽要加幾匙」的小學生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個煮一頓飯要開三個 YouTube 教學影片的朋友——我們不是不會，是沒有高級大人在旁邊我們不敢相信自己
variations: 成年人最大的謊言：「我自己會用電鍋。」
初級大人下廚：每一步都要拍照傳給媽媽確認。高級大人下廚：邊看手機邊把菜煮好。

en.content:
Junior adult cooks rice: stares at the rice cooker, googles 'how much water for two cups of rice', triple-checks the line.

Senior adult cooks rice: glances at it, sticks a finger in, done.

I make six figures, I run quarterly reports, I manage ten projects. But the second I walk into my parents' kitchen, I'm back to being a fifth-grader asking 'how many teaspoons of salt?'
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who has three YouTube tutorials open just to make dinner — we know how, we just don't trust ourselves without a senior adult nearby
variations: The biggest lie an adult tells: 'I know how to use a rice cooker.'
Junior adult cooking: texts mom a photo of every step. Senior adult cooking: scrolls phone while dinner gets made.

items.sourceNote: burnout bounce-back always-on work life balance AI era 2026 trend; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
2026 年成年人的一天：

早上 AI 幫你回信。
中午 AI 幫你寫報告。
下午 AI 幫你開會。
晚上 AI 幫你想標題。

我：「那我做什麼？」
AI：「你負責累。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個工具越來越多、人卻越來越累的同事——所謂效率，就是把同樣的時間塞更多事
variations: AI 幫我省下兩小時，公司就丟給我四小時的事。
以前累是因為事情太多，現在累是因為被期待要做更多。

en.content:
An adult's day in 2026:

Morning: AI writes your emails.
Noon: AI drafts your report.
Afternoon: AI summarizes your meeting.
Evening: AI generates your headlines.

Me: 'So what do I do?'
AI: 'You handle being tired.'
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who keeps adding tools but somehow has less time — efficiency just means cramming more into the same hours
variations: AI saves me two hours and my company immediately gives me four more hours of work.
I used to be tired because there was too much to do. Now I'm tired because there's too much I'm expected to do.

items.sourceNote: weekend recovery dread Sunday scaries adulting quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
週末的結構：

週五晚上：終於自由了。
週六早上：今天什麼都不做。
週六晚上：怎麼一天就過了。
週日中午：明天要上班了。
週日晚上：禮拜五是什麼時候的事？

所謂週末，就是兩天用來從上週復原、然後恐懼下週的緩衝期。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個禮拜天晚上都會嘆氣的朋友——我們不是懶，我們只是在做心理建設
variations: 成年人的週末公式：期待七天，享受四小時。
週五的我跟週日的我，是完全不同的兩個人。

en.content:
The anatomy of a weekend:

Friday night: finally free.
Saturday morning: doing absolutely nothing today.
Saturday night: how is the day already gone.
Sunday noon: work tomorrow.
Sunday night: was Friday a dream?

Turns out weekends are just two-day buffers to recover from last week and dread the next one.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sighs every Sunday night — we're not lazy, we're emotionally prepping
variations: Adult weekend formula: anticipate for seven days, enjoy for four hours.
Friday me and Sunday me are two completely different people.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人 看醫生 掛號 報稅 Threads viral; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人看醫生：先 google 症狀，把自己嚇到，然後打電話問媽媽要掛哪一科。

高級大人看醫生：直接走進診所，跟醫生說「就那個老問題，藥開一樣的」。

我會用 ChatGPT 寫程式、會用 AI 剪片、會用三種理財 app，但我到現在還是不知道感冒要掛家醫科還是耳鼻喉科。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個身體不舒服第一個訊息不是傳給醫生而是傳給家人群組的朋友——我們的搜尋能力很強，但生活常識是另一回事
variations: 成年人的真相：會的技能越來越多，會處理的人生卻越來越少。
初級大人報稅：每年五月都要重新學一次。高級大人報稅：十分鐘解決，順便提醒你早點交。

en.content:
Junior adult goes to the doctor: googles symptoms, panics, calls mom to ask which department to book.

Senior adult goes to the doctor: walks in and says 'same as last time, same prescription, thanks.'

I can code with ChatGPT, edit videos with AI, juggle three finance apps. But I still don't know whether a cold goes to a family doctor or an ENT.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose first move when sick is texting the family group chat, not a doctor — we're great at searching, terrible at adulting
variations: The adult truth: more skills than ever, fewer life problems we can actually solve.
Junior adult does taxes: relearns the whole thing every April. Senior adult does taxes: done in ten minutes, then reminds you to file early.

items.sourceNote: googling everything adult life quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
我以為當大人是一種狀態，
後來才發現，當大人是一種 google 搜尋紀錄。

「水費單怎麼看」
「報稅自然人憑證在哪」
「冷氣濾網多久洗一次」
「成人發燒幾度要去醫院」

沒有人教過我們這些，但只要會打字、會付網路費，你就有資格自稱大人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個搜尋紀錄裡面全是「⋯⋯怎麼辦」開頭的朋友——我們不是無知，我們只是邊查邊長大
variations: 成年人最誠實的履歷：我的瀏覽器搜尋紀錄。
當大人的第一個技能不是煮飯也不是賺錢，是學會用關鍵字 google 出答案。

en.content:
I thought being an adult was a state of being.
Turns out being an adult is just a Google search history.

'how to read a water bill'
'where do I get my tax certificate'
'how often do you clean an AC filter'
'adult fever how high before ER'

Nobody taught us this stuff. But if you can type and pay for Wi-Fi, you qualify as an adult.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose search history is 90% questions starting with 'how do I' — we're not clueless, we're just learning in real time
variations: An adult's most honest resume: their browser search history.
The first adult skill isn't cooking or earning money — it's knowing the right keywords to Google.

items.sourceNote: tired personality trait adult exhaustion quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
小時候大人問：「你長大想當什麼？」
我說：「太空人、總統、發明家。」

現在三十歲的我，每天早上躺在床上想的只有一個答案：

「我想當——累。」

累不是狀態，是我的人格特質。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天群組第一句話都是「我好累」的朋友——我們的夢想已經從拯救世界進化到只想多睡十分鐘
variations: 成年人的睡前計畫：明天我要早睡。成年人的隔天：又是凌晨一點。
我的能量條從早上就是 30%，這不是低電量模式，這是我的滿格。

en.content:
When I was a kid, adults asked: 'What do you want to be when you grow up?'
I said: 'Astronaut, president, inventor.'

Now at 30, lying in bed every morning, there's only one answer:

'I want to be — tired.'

Being tired isn't a mood. It's a personality trait.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose first message in the group chat every day is 'I'm so tired' — our dreams went from saving the world to ten more minutes of sleep
variations: Adult bedtime plan: I'll sleep early tomorrow. Adult the next night: still up at 1 AM.
My battery starts at 30% every morning. That's not low power mode. That's my full charge.

items.sourceNote: rent takes more than relationships adulting money quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
我前任沒從我身上拿走那麼多，房租倒是每個月準時來收。

感情可以淡，房東不會。
他比任何一個前任都更準時、更專一、更不會放過你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛繳完房租，戶頭比心還空的朋友——我們的房東比我們的前任還愛我們
variations: 房租是我目前維持最久的一段關係。
成年人最穩定的支出，就是房租。最不穩定的，是房租繳完以後的我。

en.content:
My ex never took as much from me as rent does, and rent shows up on time every single month.

Feelings fade. Landlords don't.
He's more punctual, more committed, and more relentless than any ex I've ever had.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose bank account is emptier than their love life on the 1st of the month — our landlords love us more than any ex ever did
variations: Rent is the longest committed relationship I've ever had.
The most stable thing in my adult life is paying rent. The most unstable thing is me right after I pay it.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人 過年回家 退化 Threads viral 2026; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
在公司：管十個人、開兩場會、簽三份合約。
回家進門五分鐘：

媽：「衣服你自己會洗嗎？」
我：「我不會。」
媽：「電鍋的水怎麼加？」
我：「我不會。」
媽：「明天幾點要叫你？」
我：「我不知道。」

外面是高級大人，回家瞬間掉到初級——而且還掉得很自願。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個過年回家立刻變回小學生的朋友——我們不是裝的，我們是真的鬆懈到不想長大
variations: 我月薪八萬，但在我媽面前，我永遠只值五十塊零用錢。
成年人最幸福的時刻：回家以後，責任暫時自動關機。

en.content:
At work: managing ten people, leading two meetings, signing three contracts.
Five minutes after walking into my parents' house:

Mom: 'Can you do your own laundry?'
Me: 'No.'
Mom: 'How much water for the rice cooker?'
Me: 'I don't know.'
Mom: 'What time should I wake you up tomorrow?'
Me: 'No idea.'

Senior adult out there. Demoted to junior the second I'm home — and very willingly.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who instantly regresses to a 10-year-old the moment they step into their parents' house — we're not faking it, we're just genuinely off duty
variations: I earn a decent salary, but in front of my mom, I'm still worth about $5 of allowance.
The best part of being an adult: the second you're home, your responsibilities auto-power-off.

items.sourceNote: weekend recovery vs dread next week adulting quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
週末存在的意義，是讓你從這禮拜緩過來，然後開始害怕下禮拜。

週六：補眠、補心情、補一切。
週日下午三點：胃開始痛。
週日晚上：開始想週一的會議。
週一早上：累得跟剛跑完馬拉松一樣。

我們不是在過週末，我們是在兩週上班之間中場休息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個禮拜天晚上開始焦慮的朋友——「週末焦慮症」是成年人的隱藏 DLC
variations: 週六剛醒，週日就準備上班；中間那個叫做休息的東西，我沒看到。
成年人的週末：48 小時的延後恐懼。

en.content:
The weekend exists so you can recover from this week and start dreading the next one.

Saturday: catch up on sleep, mood, life.
Sunday 3 PM: stomach starts hurting.
Sunday night: replaying Monday's meeting in your head.
Monday morning: tired like you just ran a marathon.

We're not having weekends. We're at halftime between two weeks of work.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who starts spiraling every Sunday at 6 PM — Sunday Scaries is the hidden DLC adulthood didn't warn us about
variations: Saturday I just woke up. Sunday I'm already prepping for work. The 'resting' part in between? Never saw it.
Adult weekends are just 48 hours of postponed dread.

items.sourceNote: 3 AM awake replaying awkward moments adulting sleep quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
晚上九點：眼皮重得像掛了兩袋米。
凌晨三點：突然清醒，想起 2014 年那次自我介紹講錯名字。

小時候大人說「失眠」是病，長大才知道，失眠是大腦免費播放你的尷尬精華片段。

我不是睡不著，我是被回憶綁架了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜三點還在群組丟訊息的朋友——成年人的大腦有自己的播放清單，全是黑歷史
variations: 白天醒不來，半夜睡不著；我的生理時鐘是壞掉的還是叛逆的？
凌晨三點的我和早上七點的我，根本是兩個人——而且兩個都很想揍對方。

en.content:
9 PM: eyelids heavy like sandbags.
3 AM: wide awake, suddenly remembering that time in 2014 you said your own name wrong during an intro.

As a kid you thought insomnia was a medical condition. As an adult you learn it's just your brain hosting a free screening of your cringiest moments.

I'm not having trouble sleeping. I'm being held hostage by memories.
en.editorNote: Tag the friend who drops random messages in the group chat at 3 AM — adult brains have their own playlist, and it's all your worst hits
variations: Can't wake up in the morning. Can't fall asleep at night. Is my body clock broken or just rebellious?
3 AM me and 7 AM me are two completely different people — and they both want to punch each other.

items.sourceNote: money in quietly leaves dramatically payday gone in 60 seconds; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
薪水：靜悄悄入帳，連個提示音都不太明顯。

各種費用：房租、水電、信用卡、訂閱、保險、媽媽我也想要一台 iPad ——全部排好隊，鳴鑼開道，敲鑼打鼓地走了。

錢進來像個內向的人，錢出去像在辦遊行。

我發薪日最快樂的時間是早上九點到九點零三分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個薪水入帳當天就變窮的朋友——成年人的快樂壽命短到可以用秒數計算
variations: 戶頭餘額和我的心一樣，月初飽滿，月底蕭條。
我不是月光族，我是「薪水入帳三秒後就月光」族。

en.content:
Salary: tiptoes into your account so quietly you almost miss the notification.

Expenses: rent, utilities, credit card, subscriptions, insurance, that thing your kid suddenly needs — all line up and march out in a full parade with drums.

Money comes in like an introvert. Money leaves like it's throwing a festival.

My happiest moment on payday lasts from 9:00 AM to 9:03 AM.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's already broke the day rent clears — adult joy has a shelf life measured in seconds
variations: My bank balance is like my mood: full at the start of the month, in ruins by the end.
I'm not living paycheck to paycheck. I'm living paycheck to three seconds after paycheck.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人/高級大人 看醫生 contrast Threads viral 2026; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人生病：先問群組「我這樣要不要看醫生」，等三個朋友都說要，才掙扎一週去診所。

高級大人生病：症狀出現當天，掛號、批價、領藥、回家，整個流程不超過兩小時。

中間那一週的差距，叫做「我以為撐一下就好」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個感冒拖到變肺炎的朋友——成年人最貴的不是醫藥費，是「再觀察看看」的那幾天
variations: 初級大人：症狀 google 三天。高級大人：掛號十分鐘。
長大的證明不是賺多少，是知道什麼時候該看醫生。

en.content:
Junior adult gets sick: posts in the group chat 'should I see a doctor for this,' waits for three friends to say yes, then drags themselves to the clinic a week later.

Senior adult gets sick: books the appointment, gets seen, pays, picks up meds, home — entire thing done in under two hours.

That week-long gap in the middle has a name: 'I thought I could tough it out.'
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose cold always turns into bronchitis — the most expensive part of adulting isn't the doctor's bill, it's the 'let me just wait and see' days
variations: Junior adult: Googles symptoms for three days. Senior adult: at the clinic in ten minutes.
Proof you've grown up isn't how much you earn — it's knowing when to actually see a doctor.

items.sourceNote: 'I need an adult — but I AM the adult' viral adulting line; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
事情爆掉的第一個反應：「快叫一個大人來處理！」

大腦花了兩秒才想起來：
喔對，我就是大人。

那兩秒是這輩子最寧靜的兩秒，因為那是最後一次有人可以救我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個遇到事情下意識想找媽媽、但媽媽也想找他的朋友——成年人的孤獨，是發現後面沒有人了
variations: 小時候出事第一句是「媽——」，長大後出事第一句還是「媽——」，只是現在媽會回你「你自己處理」。
成年人的崩潰：問題太大，但能處理的人只有我一個。

en.content:
Something goes wrong. First instinct: 'someone get an adult!'

It takes my brain two full seconds to remember:
oh right. I am the adult.

Those two seconds are the most peaceful seconds of my life — because they're the last time anyone could've saved me.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose first instinct in a crisis is still to call mom — even though mom is now calling them for the same reason
variations: As a kid the first word in a crisis was 'MOM!' As an adult the first word is still 'MOM!' — except now she texts back 'you've got this'.
The real adult meltdown isn't that the problem is too big. It's realizing you're the only one in the room who can fix it.

items.sourceNote: 'wtf am I eating for dinner every night forever' viral adulting fatigue; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人最大的詛咒不是上班，
不是繳房租，
是每天下午四點開始浮現的一個問題：

「今天晚餐到底要吃什麼？」

而且這個問題每天都會回來，
沒有解答，
沒有盡頭，
要回答到你死掉的那一天。

小時候的人生課題是「我長大要做什麼」，
現在的人生課題是「我今晚要吃什麼」。
後者比較難。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天下午都在群組丟「欸今天吃什麼」的朋友——這不是選擇障礙，這是成年人共同的詛咒
variations: 成年人的存在主義危機，發生在每天下午四點五十七分。
我可以做年度規劃、五年計畫，但我做不到回答「今天晚餐吃什麼」。

en.content:
The real curse of adulthood isn't work,
isn't rent,
it's a single question that materializes every day around 4 PM:

'What the hell am I eating for dinner tonight?'

And it comes back the next day.
And the next.
No answer, no end, forever, until you die.

As a kid the big life question was 'what do you want to be when you grow up?'
The grown-up version is 'what do you want for dinner tonight?'
The second one is harder.
en.editorNote: Tag the friend who texts the group chat 'so what are we eating' every single evening at 5 PM — this isn't indecision, it's a shared adult curse
variations: The adult existential crisis hits at exactly 4:57 PM.
I can plan a five-year career roadmap. I cannot tell you what I want for dinner in six hours.

items.sourceNote: year-long gym membership used 3 times — adult commitment vs reality; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
一月一號，下定決心：辦了一年期健身房，年繳，因為這樣比較便宜。

一月：去了四次，認真。
二月：去了一次，順便去附近吃飯。
三月到十二月：沒去。

但每個月扣款日，APP都會貼心提醒我：
「您本月還有 30 天可使用！」

我不是在健身，我是在贊助一棟我從來沒去過的建築。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個年初辦健身房、年底還沒辦理退費的朋友——成年人的決心壽命，平均三週
variations: 我每月健身房月費的真正用途，是買「我有在運動」的心理安慰。
成年人最會的兩件事：訂計畫、放棄計畫。中間相隔大概十一天。

en.content:
January 1st, full of resolve: signed up for an annual gym membership, paid upfront because it was cheaper.

January: went four times. Committed.
February: went once, mostly because the restaurant next door was good.
March through December: did not go.

But every month the app sends a cheerful reminder:
'You still have 30 days of access this month!'

I'm not working out. I'm financially supporting a building I have never set foot in.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose January gym membership is now just a quiet monthly donation — adult resolve has an average lifespan of three weeks
variations: My monthly gym fee's real purpose: buying the feeling of 'I'm being active'.
The two things adults are best at: making plans, and abandoning them. About eleven days apart.

items.sourceNote: sending memes as adult love language / staying in touch evolved form; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
我和我最好的朋友，已經三個月沒見面了。

但我們每天都在傳迷因給對方。
沒有「最近好嗎」、沒有「要不要約一下」，
就只是丟一張圖，附三個字：「你看你」。

這就是成年人的友情：
不用見面、不用聊天、不用更新近況，
只要你還會傳奇怪的東西給我，就代表你還記得我。

沉默是因為累，迷因是因為愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個三個月沒見面、但每天都在丟迷因給你的朋友——成年人的友情，已經進化成另一種形式
variations: 成年人的「我想你」，翻譯過來叫做「欸這張我覺得是你」。
見面不用，聊天不用，傳迷因不能斷——這就是大人友情的維生系統。

en.content:
My best friend and I haven't seen each other in three months.

But every single day we send each other memes.
No 'how are you', no 'we should hang out',
just an image with three words: 'this is you'.

This is adult friendship:
no meetings required, no catching up, no status updates.
As long as you keep sending me weird stuff, I know you still remember me.

Silence is because we're tired. Memes are because we love each other.
en.editorNote: Tag the friend you haven't seen in three months but somehow message every single day — adult friendship has evolved into a different format entirely
variations: 'I miss you' in adult, translated: 'lol this one's literally you'.
No need to meet, no need to talk, just don't stop sending the memes — that's the life support system of adult friendship.

items.sourceNote: browser tabs survival adulting meme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
我現在的瀏覽器分頁總共有四十七個。

其中三個是真的在工作，
兩個是「等一下要看」的文章（已經等了三個月），
一個是「我可能會買這個」的購物車，
剩下四十一個，是「我不敢關，怕關了就再也找不到了」。

這不是工作狀態，這是成年人的精神結構。
每一個分頁背後，都是一個我沒空處理的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個記憶體永遠在燒、卻一個分頁都不敢關的同事——分頁不是工作，是成年人的精神備份
variations: 成年人關不掉的不是分頁，是那些「總有一天我會看完」的幻想。
我的瀏覽器分頁，就是我人生待辦清單的視覺化呈現——一團亂、無從下手、捨不得丟。

en.content:
I currently have forty-seven browser tabs open.

Three are actual work.
Two are articles I'm 'going to read later' (it has been three months).
One is a shopping cart of things I 'might buy'.
The other forty-one I'm afraid to close because I will never find them again.

This is not a work setup. This is the architecture of my adult psyche.
Every tab represents a version of me I haven't had time to deal with.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker whose laptop fan is permanently screaming — tabs aren't work, they're an adult's offsite memory backup
variations: What adults can't close isn't tabs, it's the fantasy of 'one day I'll get to it'.
My browser tabs are a visual representation of my life's to-do list: chaotic, unreachable, impossible to throw away.

items.sourceNote: 9pm exhausted 3am awake replaying awkward moment; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人的睡眠時間表：

晚上九點：累到坐在沙發上眼睛都張不開，下定決心要早睡。
晚上十一點：洗完澡反而精神了，再看一集就睡。
凌晨一點：滑手機滑到手機掉在臉上。
凌晨三點：突然清醒，開始回想 2014 年那場聚餐我講錯的那句話。
凌晨五點：終於睡著。
早上七點：鬧鐘響。

我們不是失眠，我們是被自己的過去糾纏。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給凌晨三點突然清醒、開始回想十年前糗事的朋友——成年人的失眠，從來不是因為今天
variations: 九點想睡，十一點清醒，三點開始檢討人生——這就是大人的睡眠時間表。
我不是失眠，我是被「我十年前到底為什麼那樣講話」這個問題綁架。

en.content:
An adult's sleep schedule:

9 PM: So tired I can't keep my eyes open on the couch. Decide tonight I'm sleeping early.
10 PM: Take a shower. Inexplicably wide awake. One more episode and then bed.
1 AM: Scrolling on my phone until it drops on my face.
3 AM: Suddenly fully conscious, replaying something I said wrong at a dinner in 2014.
5 AM: Finally fall asleep.
7 AM: Alarm.

We're not insomniacs. We're being haunted by our own past.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who wakes at 3 AM replaying a cringey moment from a decade ago — adult insomnia is never about today
variations: Tired at 9, wide awake at 11, conducting a personal review of my life at 3 AM — the adult sleep schedule.
I don't have insomnia. I'm being held hostage by the question 'why did I phrase it like that ten years ago'.

items.sourceNote: money math doesn't math, big number small number contrast; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人的金錢數學是這樣的：

一千塊是：「啊買一下啦，又沒多少。」
一千塊也是：「什麼，他欠我一千塊還沒還？我們是不是該重新評估這段友情？」

同一個數字，兩種人格。
收的時候叫做巨款，花的時候叫做零頭。

這不叫亂花錢，這叫成年人的雙重標準。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月初轉很有共鳴，月底轉更有共鳴——成年人的錢，數字一樣，意義完全相反
variations: 我可以花一千塊不眨眼，但別人欠我一千塊我可以記到下輩子。
成年人最會的不是賺錢，是用兩種完全不同的標準看同一筆錢。

en.content:
Adult money math works like this:

$30 is: 'sure, throw it in, what's $30.'
$30 is also: 'wait, he still owes me $30? Do we need to reevaluate this friendship?'

Same number. Two completely different personalities.
When money comes in, it's a fortune. When it goes out, it's loose change.

This isn't reckless spending. This is the adult double standard.
en.editorNote: Hits on the 1st of the month. Hits harder on the 30th. Same number, opposite meaning.
variations: I can drop $30 without blinking. Someone owing me $30 I will remember in my next life.
Adults aren't great at making money. We're great at applying two completely different standards to the same dollar.

items.sourceNote: work is tired people in a building pretending to be fine; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
你問我上班是什麼？

上班就是：一群很累的人，在一棟很大的建築物裡，假裝自己沒事。

會議裡有人說「我們來對齊一下」，沒有人真的對齊。
Slack 裡有人說「方便的時候再回」，所有人都立刻回了。
下班前有人說「明天再聊」，意思是「永遠不要再聊」。

上班不是工作的地方，上班是成年人集體演戲的場所。
而我們，每一個人，都很會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛開完一場完全沒結論的會的同事——我們不是在工作，我們是在演
variations: 成年人最有資格拿奧斯卡的場合，是禮拜一早上十點的會議室。
工作的本質：用八小時假裝自己很 OK，然後用剩下的時間消化這場演出。

en.content:
You ask me what work is?

Work is: a bunch of very tired people, inside a very large building, pretending they're fine.

In meetings someone says 'let's align on this' and nobody aligns.
On Slack someone says 'reply whenever's convenient' and everyone replies immediately.
Before leaving someone says 'let's pick this up tomorrow' which means 'let's never pick this up'.

Work isn't where work happens. Work is where adults collectively perform.
And every single one of us is incredible at it.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who just sat through a meeting that resolved nothing — we're not working, we're acting
variations: The most Oscar-worthy adult performances happen in a conference room at 10 AM on a Monday.
The real job: spend eight hours pretending you're okay, then spend the rest of the day recovering from the performance.

items.sourceNote: let me check my calendar = polite no; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人最強的一句話，不是「我愛你」，也不是「我請客」，
是「我看一下我的行事曆」。

翻譯：我不想去。
翻譯：我不確定要不要去。
翻譯：我已經決定不去了，但我需要五秒鐘讓你以為我有在考慮。

小時候拒絕別人需要勇氣，長大後拒絕別人只需要一支手機跟兩秒鐘的假裝滑動。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是說「我看一下」然後再也沒有下文的朋友——我們不是冷漠，我們是禮貌
variations: 「我看一下我的行事曆」是成年人的暗號，意思是「我不會去，但我會體面地不去」。
成年後最重要的技能不是談判，是學會用「再看看」結束一個邀約。

en.content:
The most powerful sentence in adulthood isn't 'I love you' or 'it's on me' —
it's 'let me check my calendar.'

Translation: I don't want to go.
Translation: I'm not sure I want to go.
Translation: I've already decided I'm not going, but I need five seconds to make you believe I considered it.

As a kid, saying no took courage. As an adult, it just takes a phone and two seconds of fake scrolling.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always says 'let me check' and then ghosts the invite — we're not cold, we're just polite
variations: 'Let me check my calendar' is the adult code for 'I'm not coming, but I'll decline with dignity'.
The most important adult skill isn't negotiation — it's learning to end an invitation with 'I'll get back to you'.

items.sourceNote: 3 AM replaying awkward memory adult insomnia tweet; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人的睡眠規律：

晚上九點：眼皮重到睜不開，差點在沙發上斷氣。
上床十二點：完全清醒。
凌晨三點：突然想起 2014 年某天你跟同學說了一句很奇怪的話。

身體想睡，腦袋不肯。腦袋裡播的不是夢，是你過去十年的尷尬精選輯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜三點還在回想國中糗事的朋友——你不孤單，整個成年人世界都醒著
variations: 成年人最忠實的陪伴不是寵物，是凌晨三點突然冒出來的黑歷史。
小時候怕鬼，長大後怕的是自己腦袋裡的記憶庫。

en.content:
Adult sleep schedule:

9 PM: eyelids so heavy you almost flatline on the couch.
Midnight in bed: wide awake.
3 AM: suddenly remember that weird thing you said to a classmate in 2014.

Body wants sleep. Brain refuses. What's playing in there isn't a dream — it's a curated highlight reel of every cringe moment from the last decade.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still replaying middle school memories at 3 AM — you're not alone, the entire adult world is awake too
variations: An adult's most loyal companion isn't a pet — it's the random embarrassing memory that ambushes you at 3 AM.
As a kid I was scared of ghosts. As an adult I'm scared of the archive in my own head.

items.sourceNote: marriage texting do we need milk tweet; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
小時候以為結婚是浪漫的事。

後來才發現，結婚的本質就是：
兩個人輪流傳訊息問對方「家裡還有沒有衛生紙」、「醬油要不要買」、「冰箱裡那個東西可以丟了嗎」，
一直問到其中一個離開為止。

婚姻不是日劇，婚姻是一個共用購物清單的長期合作關係。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛剛在群組裡問另一半「要不要買蛋」的朋友——浪漫不是不在了，是進化成了補貨提醒
variations: 婚姻的真相：兩個人每天討論的不是未來，是廚房紙巾。
戀愛時聊星座，結婚後聊水電費——這就是成長。

en.content:
As a kid you thought marriage was romantic.

Turns out marriage is mostly:
two people taking turns texting each other 'do we need toilet paper', 'should I grab soy sauce', 'can I throw away that thing in the fridge' —
until one of them leaves.

Marriage isn't a drama. Marriage is a long-term partnership built on a shared shopping list.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just texted their partner 'do we need eggs' — romance isn't dead, it just evolved into restock alerts
variations: The truth about marriage: the daily topic isn't the future, it's paper towels.
Dating talk: zodiac signs. Married talk: the electric bill. That's growth.

items.sourceNote: everyone is just winging it no adult handbook quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
小時候看大人，覺得他們什麼都會。

好像有一本《人生使用說明書》，只要滿十八歲就會自動拿到。

長大後才發現，沒有那本書。
從來沒有。

大家都在邊走邊查，邊查邊裝沒事，
然後互相點頭說「對啊，這個我懂」——
其實每個人心裡都在偷偷 google。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在懷疑「別人是不是都比我懂事」的朋友——我們都一樣，差別只在誰演得比較像
variations: 成年人最大的共識：沒有人真的知道自己在幹嘛。
小時候以為大人是另一個物種，長大後才知道，我們只是長高了的小孩。

en.content:
As a kid, adults looked like they knew everything.

Like there was some 'How to Do Life' handbook that automatically showed up at 18.

Then you grow up and realize: there's no handbook.
There never was.

Everyone's just figuring it out as they go, faking it in the meantime,
and nodding at each other like 'oh yeah, totally, I get it' —
while secretly googling under the table.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who quietly wonders if everyone else has it more together — we're all in the same boat, just different acting skills
variations: The biggest adult consensus: nobody actually knows what they're doing.
As a kid I thought adults were a different species. Turns out we're just taller children.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人/高級大人 拜拜場景延伸；rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人拜拜：手裡拿著香，站在旁邊偷瞄媽媽，看她插哪根才敢插哪根。

高級大人拜拜：知道哪一爐先拜、要拜幾下、心裡默念的順序是「祖先、地基主、然後拜託這個月信用卡帳單可以少一點」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次回家拜拜都要被媽媽碎念「香怎麼拿成這樣」的朋友——我們不是不孝，我們只是技術尚未升級
variations: 初級大人過年：被問薪水只會傻笑。高級大人過年：先發紅包堵住所有長輩的嘴。
初級大人拜拜：金紙不知道要折。高級大人拜拜：金紙折得比廟公還整齊。

en.content:
Junior adult at a family ritual: holds the incense, side-eyes mom, waits to see which urn she uses first before daring to copy.

Senior adult at a family ritual: knows which altar comes first, how many bows, and which silent prayer to say — usually 'ancestors, kitchen god, and please let this month's credit card bill be smaller.'
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who gets scolded every holiday for 'holding the incense wrong' — we're not disrespectful, just technically un-upgraded
variations: Junior adult at New Year: just smiles when relatives ask about salary. Senior adult: hands out red envelopes first to shut everyone up.
Junior adult: doesn't know how to fold the offering paper. Senior adult: folds it neater than the temple keeper.

items.sourceNote: millennial laundry chair meme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人的家裡都有一張椅子。

那張椅子不是用來坐的。
是用來放「洗好但還沒折」、「穿過但還不算髒」、「等一下要穿但現在不想掛回去」的衣服。

那張椅子才是真正的衣櫃。
衣櫃只是給客人看的展示空間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡也有一張「衣服椅」的朋友——這不是亂，這是動態收納系統
variations: 成年人的衣櫃叫做「那張椅子」，那張椅子叫做「我等等會整理」。
判斷一個人有沒有長大，不是看他幾歲，是看他家裡那張椅子上的衣服堆得多高。

en.content:
Every adult has a chair at home.

That chair is not for sitting.
It's for clothes that are 'washed but not folded', 'worn but not dirty', and 'I'll put it back later but not right now'.

That chair is the real closet.
The actual closet is just a display case for guests.
en.editorNote: Tag the friend whose bedroom chair has its own zip code — it's not mess, it's a dynamic storage system
variations: An adult's wardrobe is called 'that chair', and that chair is called 'I'll sort it later'.
You don't measure adulthood in years — you measure it in how high the clothes pile on that one chair.

items.sourceNote: adult money goes to maintenance quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
小時候以為長大後的錢會花在：
旅遊、咖啡廳、漂亮的家、想買就買的小奢侈。

長大後發現錢都花在：
冷氣保養、機車驗車、隱形眼鏡、清潔劑、保險自付額、
還有「上次那個東西壞了又要換一個」。

大人的錢，九成都拿去維持現狀。
剩下那一成，拿來買飲料安慰自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個月底看帳單會嘆氣的朋友——你不是亂花，你只是在維持人生不要崩塌
variations: 成年人的錢包：90% 維修費，9% 飲料費，1% 對未來的幻想。
小時候以為薪水會變多就會自由，長大後才知道，薪水是用來補洞的。

en.content:
As a kid you thought adult money would go to:
travel, cute cafés, a beautiful home, little luxuries on a whim.

Then you grow up and the money actually goes to:
AC servicing, car inspections, contact lenses, cleaning supplies, insurance deductibles,
and 'that thing broke again, gotta replace it'.

90% of adult money goes to maintaining the status quo.
The other 10% goes to a drink to comfort yourself.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sighs at every month-end statement — you're not overspending, you're just keeping life from collapsing
variations: Adult wallet: 90% maintenance, 9% beverages, 1% fantasies about the future.
As a kid you thought a bigger paycheck would mean freedom. Turns out paychecks are just for patching holes.

items.sourceNote: adulting is googling how to do things tweet; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
長大的本質就是：

一邊 google「這個東西要怎麼弄」，
一邊在會議上點頭說「對對對我知道」，
一邊祈禱沒有人會追問細節。

然後等對方走了，趕快再回去 google 第二次，
因為你連剛剛 google 到的答案都還沒看懂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛剛在會議上裝懂、現在正在偷偷查資料的同事——你不是唯一一個
variations: 成年人的超能力不是經驗，是即時 google 加面無表情。
工作三年的我跟工作三天的我，差別只在 google 速度比較快。

en.content:
The essence of being an adult:

Googling 'how do you do this thing',
while nodding in a meeting saying 'yeah yeah I got it',
while praying nobody asks follow-up questions.

Then the moment they leave, you go back and google it a second time —
because you still don't understand the first answer you found.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who just nodded knowingly in a meeting and is now frantically searching — you are not alone
variations: An adult's superpower isn't experience — it's googling in real time with a straight face.
Three years on the job vs three days on the job: the only difference is google speed.

items.sourceNote: 'adulting is soup, and I'm a fork' viral 2026 line; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人的日常感受：

人生是一碗湯，
而我手上拿的是一支叉子。

不是我不努力，
是這個工具從一開始就不對。
但沒有人會換給你，
所以你只能繼續戳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得自己「明明很認真但一切都很卡」的朋友——不是你不行，是工具配錯了
variations: 成年人的核心困境：工具不對，但你還是被要求要吃完。
長大以後你才懂：很多事情不是不會做，是根本沒給你對的工具。

en.content:
Daily adult experience:

Life is a bowl of soup,
and I'm holding a fork.

It's not that I'm not trying.
The tool was wrong from the start.
But nobody's swapping it out for you,
so you just keep stabbing.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying 'I'm trying so hard but nothing works' — it's not you, the toolkit was wrong
variations: The core adult dilemma: wrong tool, still expected to finish the bowl.
Growing up means realizing some things aren't impossible — you were just never handed the right utensil.

items.sourceNote: 'my professional brand is shows up and tries' millennial viral 2026; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
我的職場人設：

不是最聰明的、
不是最會說話的、
不是最有想法的，

是「有來，有試」。

別小看這四個字，
上班十年我才發現，
光是穩定出現在會議室、
還願意點開資料夾，
就已經贏了一半的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天覺得自己不夠厲害的朋友——你出現了，就是一種貢獻
variations: 成年人的勝利公式：別人不來的時候你來了，別人放棄的時候你還在試。
職場真相：才華是加分，穩定出現才是基本盤。

en.content:
My professional brand is:

not the smartest,
not the most charismatic,
not the one with the boldest ideas —

it's 'shows up and tries'.

Don't underestimate those four words.
Ten years in, I figured out
that just being reliably present in the meeting room
and willing to open the doc
already puts you ahead of half the room.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels like they're not impressive enough at work — showing up is itself a contribution
variations: Adult winning formula: showing up when others don't, still trying when others quit.
Workplace truth: talent is a bonus. Reliably showing up is the baseline.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人 天然氣檢查 27歲家裡沒大人 viral Threads 2026; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
天然氣公司打電話來說要上門檢查。

二十七歲的我，第一個反應是：
「請問可以晚一點嗎？家裡現在沒有大人。」

講完才驚覺——
等等，我就是大人。

初級大人最大的危機，
是有時候連自己已經長大這件事，都會忘記。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個獨居後第一次接到師傅上門電話、瞬間想找媽媽的朋友
variations: 初級大人的反射動作：被問「家裡有人嗎？」第一個反應是「我要去叫媽媽」，三秒後才想起自己住的是套房。
二十七歲的覺悟：原來「家裡的大人」這個位置，現在輪到我了。

en.content:
The gas company called to schedule an inspection at my place.

At 27, my first instinct was:
'Could you come later? There's no adult home right now.'

Then I froze — wait.
I'm the adult.

The biggest junior adult crisis
is sometimes forgetting that you already grew up.
en.editorNote: For the friend who just moved out and instinctively wants to call mom every time someone shows up at the door
variations: Junior adult reflex: someone asks 'is there an adult home?' and you say 'let me get my mom' — then remember you live in a studio.
Realization at 27: the 'adult of the house' role apparently transferred to me when I wasn't looking.

items.sourceNote: Gen-Z 'haven't eaten in 9 hours but I'm fine, still scrolling' viral 2026; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人的奇怪能力：

可以九個小時沒吃東西，覺得自己沒事。
可以連續滑手機兩小時，覺得自己在休息。
可以一整天沒講話，覺得自己很OK。

身體在求救，腦袋說「等一下」，
手機說「再看一個就好」。

三方持續談判，沒有人贏。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一整天沒喝水、一直說「等等」的朋友，順便提醒他去倒杯水
variations: 成年人的肚子：在抗議。成年人的腦袋：在當沒聽到。成年人的手指：繼續往下滑。
我不是不照顧自己，我只是在跟自己進行漫長的協商。

en.content:
Strange adult abilities:

Can go nine hours without eating and feel fine.
Can scroll for two straight hours and call it 'resting'.
Can go a whole day without speaking and feel totally OK.

Body is signaling for help. Brain says 'in a minute'.
Phone says 'just one more video'.

Three-way negotiation, ongoing, no winners.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who hasn't drunk water all day and keeps saying 'in a sec' — also remind them to drink the water
variations: Adult stomach: filing a complaint. Adult brain: pretending not to hear it. Adult thumb: keeps scrolling.
I'm not neglecting myself. I'm just in an extended negotiation with myself that's been going on for hours.

items.sourceNote: 'waiting for adult personality to arrive via Amazon Prime' viral 2026 line; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
我一直在等那個「真正的大人版本的我」上線。

你知道，就是那種：
會自己安排健檢、會準時繳稅、知道牙膏快用完要先補一條的人。

我等了好幾年，
後來才發現——
他不會自己出現。

那個版本，是要你親自把他養出來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總覺得「等我變成大人後就會……」的朋友——你就是那個大人，只是還在裝載中
variations: 我以為長大會像下載軟體，時間到就自動安裝完成。結果它是手動編譯，還會跳錯誤訊息。
成熟不是某天突然降臨的禮物，是你每天偷偷練習出來的肌肉。

en.content:
I keep waiting for the 'real adult version of me' to come online.

You know — the one who books their own physicals, pays taxes on time, notices the toothpaste is almost out and buys a spare.

I waited for years.
Then I realized:
he's not showing up on his own.

That version of me has to be raised by hand. By me.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps saying 'once I become an actual adult I'll...' — you are the adult. Still loading.
variations: I thought adulthood would download itself like a software update. Turns out it compiles manually and throws errors.
Maturity isn't a gift that arrives one day. It's a muscle you build in secret, one rep at a time.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人 報稅季求救 Threads 2026 viral angle; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人報稅：打開系統，看到「請選擇您的扣繳憑單」就先關掉，深呼吸三次，再打電話問會計朋友。

高級大人報稅：在通勤路上五分鐘搞定，順便算了今年該不該換房子。

同樣是繳稅，差距是十年資歷。
zh-tw.editorNote: 報稅季傳給那個又在群組哀嚎「我不會用」的朋友——你不孤單，整個初級大人聯盟都在哀嚎
variations: 初級大人報稅口訣：先深呼吸、再打給媽媽、最後問會計朋友、實在不行就直接放棄爭取退稅。
高級大人不是不怕報稅，是知道再怕也要按下「送出」這個按鈕。

en.content:
Junior adult doing taxes: opens the portal, sees 'please select your withholding form', closes the tab, takes three deep breaths, then calls a friend who works in accounting.

Senior adult doing taxes: finishes it in five minutes during the commute, and also runs the math on whether to refinance this year.

Same tax form. Ten-year experience gap.
en.editorNote: Send during tax season to the friend already wailing in the group chat — you're not alone, the whole junior adult alliance is wailing with you
variations: Junior adult tax-season survival kit: deep breath, call mom, text the accountant friend, and if all else fails, give up on the refund.
Senior adults aren't fearless about taxes. They've just made peace with hitting 'submit' anyway.

items.sourceNote: 'weekend exists to recover and dread' viral 2026 line; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人的週末由三部分組成：

星期五晚上——「終於放假了！」（在沙發上不動。）
星期六——「明天還有一天！」（在床上不動。）
星期日晚上——「明天就要上班了。」（什麼都不想動，但開始焦慮。）

所謂週末，其實是一段在沙發、床、和焦慮之間反覆切換的儀式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上傳給那個正在「Sunday scaries」的朋友——這不是你的問題，這是制度設計
variations: 成年人的週末公式：休息時間 = 真正放鬆的時間 − 想到星期一的時間。通常等於零。
週末不是兩天，是「一天放鬆 + 一天倒數」。我們都是這樣過來的。

en.content:
An adult's weekend has three parts:

Friday night — 'finally, freedom!' (doesn't move from the couch.)
Saturday — 'one more whole day to go!' (doesn't move from the bed.)
Sunday night — 'I have to work tomorrow.' (still doesn't move, but starts panicking.)

What we call 'a weekend' is just a ritual of rotating between the couch, the bed, and existential dread.
en.editorNote: Send on Sunday evening to the friend deep in the Sunday scaries — it's not a personal flaw, it's the system
variations: Adult weekend formula: rest time = actual relaxation − time spent thinking about Monday. Usually equals zero.
A weekend isn't two days. It's 'one day of rest + one day of countdown'. We all live this way.

items.sourceNote: millennial 'therapy is maintenance not failure' viral 2026 mental health angle; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
以前覺得去諮商代表自己「不行了」。

長大後才懂——
諮商就跟洗牙、做健檢、保養機車一樣，
是定期保養，不是出事才要去。

你不會等車子爆缸才換機油，
那為什麼要等自己崩潰才願意找人聊聊？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在猶豫「我有需要去諮商嗎」的朋友——保養不是承認壞掉，是不想讓它壞掉
variations: 高級大人的覺悟：照顧情緒跟保養機車是同一件事，只是你不會跟人說你最近去做了「心理的五千公里保養」。
心理健康跟換機油的差別只有一個：機油到了就會提醒你，情緒不會。

en.content:
I used to think going to therapy meant 'I'm broken'.

Then I grew up.
Therapy is like getting your teeth cleaned, getting a check-up, getting your car serviced —
it's maintenance, not damage control.

You don't wait for your engine to blow up before changing the oil.
So why are you waiting for yourself to fall apart before talking to someone?
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still wondering 'do I really need therapy though' — maintenance isn't admitting it's broken, it's keeping it from breaking
variations: Senior adult realization: looking after your emotions is the same as servicing your car — except you don't tell people you're going in for your '5,000-mile mental tune-up'.
The only difference between mental health and an oil change: the car will tell you when it's due. Your feelings won't.

items.sourceNote: adulting = booking your own appointments feels like an achievement viral theme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
我今天自己打電話預約了牙醫。

不是別人提醒我，不是媽媽幫我打，是我自己，主動，撥了那通電話。

做完之後我盯著手機看了三秒，覺得自己像個真正的大人，
然後決定這份成就感可以撐我一整個禮拜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個光是「自己約好健檢」就想頒獎給自己的朋友，這份驕傲完全合理
variations: 大人的成就解鎖清單：自己約看診、自己領藥、自己記得回診——每一項都值得開香檳。
別人慶祝升遷，我慶祝我終於把拖了三個月的健檢約好了。標準不同，但快樂是真的。

en.content:
I booked my own dentist appointment today.

Nobody reminded me. My mom didn't call for me. I did it. On purpose. I dialed the number myself.

Afterwards I stared at my phone for three seconds, felt like a genuine adult,
and decided this sense of accomplishment was going to carry me for an entire week.
en.editorNote: For the friend who wants a medal just for scheduling a check-up — honestly, the pride is valid
variations: Adult achievement unlocked list: booked own appointment, picked up own meds, remembered own follow-up — each one deserves champagne.
Other people celebrate promotions. I'm celebrating finally scheduling the check-up I'd put off for three months. Different bar, same joy.

items.sourceNote: adulting = canceling plans you were excited to make viral relatable theme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人：答應了聚會，當天硬撐著去，全程很想回家。

高級大人：答應的當下就知道自己不會去，但還是先答應，因為取消的快樂要留到當天才釋放。

人生最大的小確幸，就是對方比你先說「不如改天」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個跟你一樣，最開心的瞬間是聚會被取消的朋友，互相理解的默契
variations: 成年人的友情：兩個人都鬆一口氣的那種取消，才是真正的雙贏。
答應聚會的我，跟當天想取消的我，永遠不是同一個人。

en.content:
Junior adult: says yes to plans, forces themselves to show up, spends the whole time wanting to go home.

Senior adult: knows at the moment of saying yes that they won't go, but says yes anyway, because the joy of canceling is best saved for the day itself.

Life's greatest small win: when the other person texts 'rain check?' before you do.
en.editorNote: For the friend whose happiest moment is a canceled hangout — mutual understanding, no judgment
variations: Adult friendship: the kind of cancellation where both of you sigh with relief is the real win-win.
The me who agrees to plans and the me who wants to cancel on the day are never the same person.

items.sourceNote: adulting = 'we should hang out soon' for months viral friendship theme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
成年人的友情，是兩個人連續半年互相說「最近找個時間聚一下」，
然後一次都沒有真的聚到，
但彼此都很確定——這段友情牢不可破。

我們不是疏遠了，我們只是都很忙著各自累。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你一直說要約、卻已經半年沒見的好朋友，順便這次真的把時間敲下來
variations: 「下次一定要約」是成年人之間最真誠的謊言，因為說的時候是真心的。
大人的友情不用常常見面，只要哪天真的見到，還是可以從上次中斷的地方繼續聊。

en.content:
Adult friendship is two people saying 'we should hang out soon' for six straight months,
never actually managing to,
but both being completely certain the friendship is unbreakable.

We didn't drift apart. We're just both busy being tired separately.
en.editorNote: Send to the good friend you keep meaning to see but haven't in six months — then actually pick a date this time
variations: 'We should definitely hang out' is the most sincere lie between adults, because we genuinely mean it when we say it.
Adult friendship doesn't need frequent meetups — just that when you finally do meet, you pick up right where you left off.

items.sourceNote: adulting = energy-saving mode / always tired / avocado prices viral theme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
我不是懶，我只是長期處於「省電模式」。

螢幕亮度調到最低，背景程式全部關掉，能不動就不動，
只為了把那一格電留給真正重要的事——
比如下班、發薪日，還有偶爾不用看價錢就買得起酪梨的那一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總說「我不是懶我是在省電」的朋友，大人的累是會累積的
variations: 大人的目標很樸實：有天能不看價錢就把酪梨放進購物車。
別說我擺爛，我是把有限的電量留給最低限度的生存。

en.content:
I'm not lazy. I'm just permanently in 'low-power mode'.

Screen brightness at minimum, background apps all closed, move only when absolutely necessary,
all to save that last bar of battery for the things that actually matter —
like clocking out, payday, and the rare day I can buy avocados without checking the price first.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps saying 'I'm not lazy, I'm conserving energy' — adult tiredness compounds
variations: Adult goals are humble: one day, putting avocados in the cart without looking at the price.
I'm not slacking. I'm rationing a limited battery for the bare minimum of survival.

items.sourceNote: adulting = wild night is being in bed by 9 / excited to go home early viral theme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人的瘋狂夜晚：續攤到天亮，隔天再來補眠。

高級大人的瘋狂夜晚：九點躺上床，邊充電邊滑手機，十點準時關燈。

以前最怕別人說「這麼早就回家喔」，
現在聽到只覺得：對啊，是不是很爽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個現在最大的快樂是早早回家躺平的朋友，這不是變無聊，是進化
variations: 長大後對「狂歡」的定義：洗完澡、敷個面膜、十點前躺上床，明天還能睡到自然醒。
初級大人怕被說早睡，高級大人主動炫耀：「我昨天九點半就睡了。」

en.content:
Junior adult's wild night: out until sunrise, recovering with a nap the next day.

Senior adult's wild night: in bed by 9, scrolling while it charges, lights out at 10 sharp.

I used to dread someone saying 'leaving already? so early.'
Now I just think: yeah, and isn't it great.
en.editorNote: For the friend whose biggest joy is getting home early to lie down — that's not boring, that's evolution
variations: Adult definition of 'partying hard': shower done, face mask on, in bed before 10, and able to sleep in tomorrow.
Junior adult dreads being called an early sleeper. Senior adult brags about it: 'I was asleep by 9:30 last night.'

items.sourceNote: 'being tired is now a personality trait' viral adulting theme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
小時候填自我介紹，興趣寫畫畫、運動、看書。

現在如果要老實寫，興趣那欄只剩一個字：累。

不是做了什麼才累，是醒著就已經在消耗了。
「累」已經默默變成我的人格特質。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被問興趣只想回「睡覺」的朋友，誠實到讓人笑出來
variations: 別人問我最近在忙什麼，我也不知道，反正就是很累。忙著累，大概是這樣。

en.content:
As a kid, the 'hobbies' box on every form said: drawing, sports, reading.

If I filled it out honestly now, there'd be one word left: tired.

Not tired from doing anything — just tired from being awake.
Somewhere along the way, 'tired' quietly became a personality trait.
en.editorNote: For the friend whose honest answer to 'what are your hobbies' is just 'sleeping' — send without comment
variations: People ask what I've been up to. I don't know either. I'm just tired. Busy being tired, I guess.

items.sourceNote: 'apologies for the late reply / email guilt' viral adulting theme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人回訊息：已讀了三天，每天都想著「等等回」，然後愧疚地一直放著。

高級大人回訊息：看到當下決定——重要的馬上回，不重要的直接放生，不放在心上。

那份「我等等再回」的罪惡感，才是真正壓垮初級大人的東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個收件匣有五十封未回、每封都在折磨他良心的朋友
variations: 初級大人最大的工作量，不是要回的訊息，是「還沒回的訊息」帶來的心理負擔。

en.content:
Junior adult replying to messages: leaves them on read for three days, thinks 'I'll get to it later' every single day, lets the guilt quietly pile up.

Senior adult replying to messages: decides on the spot — important ones get a reply now, the rest get released into the wild, no second thoughts.

That 'I'll reply later' guilt is the thing actually crushing junior adults.
en.editorNote: For the friend with fifty unanswered messages, each one personally haunting their conscience
variations: A junior adult's heaviest workload isn't the messages they have to answer — it's the mental weight of the ones they haven't.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人測驗 / 電話恐懼 viral 2026 angle; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人要打電話訂位、預約看診、打給客服：
先在心裡演練三遍對方會說什麼、自己要怎麼回，深呼吸，按下撥號，響第一聲就想掛掉。

高級大人要打電話：直接打。講完掛掉。下一件事。

能不能輕鬆打出一通電話，是初級大人和高級大人之間最遠的距離。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個寧願走去現場也不想打電話的朋友，懂的都懂
variations: 初級大人的成就解鎖：今天主動打了一通電話，沒有先傳訊息問「方便講電話嗎」。

en.content:
Junior adult making a phone call — for a reservation, a doctor's appointment, customer service:
rehearses the whole conversation three times in their head, takes a deep breath, hits dial, and wants to hang up on the first ring.

Senior adult making a call: just calls. Says the thing. Hangs up. Next task.

Being able to casually make a phone call is the longest distance between a junior adult and a senior one.
en.editorNote: For the friend who'd rather walk to the place in person than make a two-minute phone call — no judgment
variations: Junior adult achievement unlocked: made a phone call today without first texting 'is now a good time to call?'

items.sourceNote: 初級大人 飯鍋水量/煮飯不熟 viral 2026 example; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人煮飯：盯著電鍋的內鍋，米加好了，水到底要加到哪一條線？拿手機拍下來傳給媽媽：「這樣對嗎？」

高級大人煮飯：用手指比一下水位，剛剛好，不用量，不用問。

那條看不見的水位線，就是初級大人和高級大人之間的那道牆。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次煮飯都要視訊問媽媽水量的朋友，這就是經典款初級大人
variations: 初級大人的廚房求生守則：所有問題的最終解答，都是一通打給媽媽的電話。

en.content:
Junior adult cooking rice: stares into the rice cooker, rice is in, but how far up does the water go? Takes a photo, sends it to mom: 'Is this right?'

Senior adult cooking rice: dips a finger to check the water level, perfect, no measuring, no asking.

That invisible water line is the exact wall between a junior adult and a senior one.
en.editorNote: For the friend who video-calls their mom every time they cook rice — peak classic junior adult behavior
variations: Junior adult kitchen survival rule: the final answer to every question is a phone call to mom.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人 過年圍爐被分到細路枱 / 飯桌座位 viral 2026 example; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
三十歲了，年夜飯回到家，
大圓桌坐滿了，你被安排到旁邊那張矮一階的小桌。

你有工作、有房租、有勞健保，
但在這張桌子上，你還是那個不能決定幾點走的小孩。

座位高度，就是大人等級的刻度。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個過年永遠被分到小桌、菜還是別桌先夾走的朋友，誠實到想笑
variations: 高級大人坐主桌，順手幫你夾菜。初級大人坐小桌，等著被夾菜。這就是江湖地位。

en.content:
You're 30. You go home for the family reunion dinner.
The big round table is full, so you get seated at the lower little table off to the side.

You have a job, rent, insurance —
but at this table, you're still the kid who can't decide what time to leave.

Seat height is the official measuring stick of adult rank.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's still relegated to the kids' table at every family gathering — painfully real
variations: Senior adults sit at the main table and pile food onto your plate. Junior adults sit at the side table and wait to be served. This is the pecking order.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人 買生果要問高級大人 / 不會挑水果 viral 2026 example; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人去買水果：站在攤子前，拿起一顆，又放下，再拿起另一顆，看不出哪顆甜、哪顆熟，最後偷偷傳訊息問家裡的高級大人。

高級大人去買水果：拿起來聞一下、敲兩下、捏一捏，挑好就走。

會不會挑水果，是初級大人這輩子最想偷學卻學不會的技能。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次買水果都像在拆炸彈的朋友，認命的笑容你懂的
variations: 初級大人選西瓜：用手機Google「怎麼挑西瓜」，敲一敲，假裝聽得懂那個聲音。

en.content:
Junior adult buying fruit: stands at the stall, picks one up, puts it down, picks up another, can't tell which is sweet or ripe, and ends up secretly texting the senior adult back home.

Senior adult buying fruit: gives it a sniff, a couple of taps, a gentle squeeze, picks the right one, and walks off.

Knowing how to pick good fruit is the skill junior adults most want to steal and somehow never can.
en.editorNote: For the friend who treats every trip to the produce aisle like defusing a bomb — that resigned little smile says it all
variations: Junior adult picking a watermelon: Googles 'how to choose a watermelon,' taps it twice, pretends to understand what the sound means.

items.sourceNote: 'being tired is now a personality trait' viral 2026 line; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
小時候有興趣、有夢想、有喜歡的東西。

長大後別人問你：「你平常喜歡做什麼？」
你想了三秒，誠實地回答：「睡覺。還有，累。」

累不再是一種狀態，
它已經升級成你的人格特質了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被問「興趣是什麼」會卡住的朋友，現代成年人的標準症狀
variations: 個人簡介更新：興趣——休息。專長——想休息但沒空休息。目前狀態——累。

en.content:
As a kid you had hobbies, dreams, things you loved.

As an adult, when someone asks 'what do you like to do for fun?'
you think for three seconds and answer honestly: 'Sleep. And being tired.'

Tired isn't a temporary state anymore.
It's been upgraded to a permanent personality trait.
en.editorNote: For the friend who freezes when asked about their hobbies — a standard symptom of the modern adult
variations: Bio updated: Interests — resting. Skills — wanting to rest but having no time to rest. Current status — tired.

items.sourceNote: 'adulting means choosing which bill to panic over first' viral 2026 line; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
小時候以為當大人就是想買什麼就買什麼。

長大後才發現，當大人是——
一堆帳單同時到，錢只夠付一半，
你要冷靜地決定：這個月先讓哪一張焦慮你。

這不是理財，這是分流式恐慌管理。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底前傳，給那個正在排序帳單優先級的朋友會心一笑
variations: 成年人的選擇題：水費、電費、信用卡——只能先救一個，剩下的下個月再說。這題沒有標準答案，只有比較不痛的答案。

en.content:
As a kid you thought being an adult meant buying whatever you wanted.

Turns out being an adult means:
a stack of bills all arrive at once, you can only cover half,
and you calmly decide which one gets to stress you out first this month.

This isn't budgeting. It's triage-based panic management.
en.editorNote: Send before the end of the month — the friend ranking their bills by urgency will smile knowingly
variations: Adult multiple choice: water, electricity, credit card — you can only rescue one, the rest are next month's problem. There's no right answer, just the least painful one.

items.sourceNote: 'I can't do my taxes but I know the mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell' viral classroom-vs-life line; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
學校教了你細胞裡的粒線體、勾股定理、唐宋八大家，

但沒有人教你怎麼報稅、怎麼看懂保單、怎麼跟房東談租約、生病要掛哪一科。

長大後你才發現，
那張畢業證書，附的不是說明書，是考卷。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給第一次自己報稅、對著表格發呆的朋友，全成年人共同的痛
variations: 成年人的悲哀：背得出元素週期表，但不知道綜所稅扣除額怎麼算。學校的重點，跟人生的重點，好像有點對不上。

en.content:
School taught you the mitochondria, the Pythagorean theorem, and the great poets.

Nobody taught you how to file taxes, read an insurance policy, negotiate a lease, or figure out which clinic to go to when you're sick.

Growing up, you realize
the diploma didn't come with an instruction manual.
It came with a pop quiz.
en.editorNote: For the friend staring blankly at a tax form for the first time — a pain shared by all adults everywhere
variations: The tragedy of adulthood: can recite the periodic table, has no idea how tax deductions work. What school emphasized and what life requires don't quite line up.

items.sourceNote: 'exhausted at 9 PM, wide awake at 3 AM replaying an awkward moment' viral adulting line; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
長大後的睡眠很妙：

晚上九點——累到眼睛睜不開，倒在沙發上像被拔掉插頭。
凌晨三點——突然清醒，腦袋自動播放你十年前講錯的那句話。

身體想睡的時候腦袋不睡，腦袋想睡的時候已經天亮了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜會突然回想起國中尷尬往事的朋友，凌晨三點傳最有殺傷力
variations: 初級大人的失眠不是睡不著，是大腦堅持要幫你複習這輩子所有的社死現場。

en.content:
Adult sleep is a strange thing:

9 PM — so tired your eyes won't stay open, you collapse on the couch like someone pulled your plug.
3 AM — suddenly wide awake, your brain auto-playing that thing you said wrong ten years ago.

Your body wants sleep when your brain won't, and your brain finally relents right as the sun comes up.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose brain replays middle-school embarrassments at night — maximum impact at 3 AM
variations: Junior adult insomnia isn't being unable to sleep. It's your brain insisting on reviewing every socially mortifying moment of your entire life.

items.sourceNote: 'adult money goes to unglamorous maintenance costs' viral theme; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
小時候以為大人的錢都花在很酷的東西上：旅行、車子、名牌。

長大才發現，大人的錢大部分都花在「維持現狀」：
看牙、修水管、換輪胎、補保險、買清潔劑。

沒有一樣會讓你開心，但少一樣你的人生就會崩塌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛繳完一筆「不得不花」的維修費、心在淌血的朋友
variations: 成年人的消費分兩種：想買的，和不買不行的。可惜薪水大部分都被第二種吃掉了。

en.content:
As a kid you thought adult money went to cool things: travel, cars, designer stuff.

Turns out most adult money goes to 'keeping things from falling apart':
dentist visits, plumbing, new tires, insurance, cleaning supplies.

None of it makes you happy, but skip any one of them and your whole life caves in.
en.editorNote: For the friend who just paid an unavoidable repair bill and is quietly bleeding inside
variations: Adult spending comes in two kinds: things you want, and things you have no choice about. Sadly, most of the paycheck goes to the second kind.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人 港台爆紅: 飲茶坐細路枱/買生果要問高級大人; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
初級大人家庭聚餐：負責顧小孩、跑腿加湯、被分配坐在最角落那一桌。

高級大人家庭聚餐：坐主位、決定要不要加菜、結帳時揮揮手說「我來」。

你已經出社會十年，但在這張圓桌上，你的座位永遠靠廁所最近。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個過年圍爐永遠被安排在小孩桌的朋友，三十歲還在等升級
variations: 初級大人的家族地位指標：聚餐時你還是那個被叫去櫃檯加白飯、續茶水的人，而不是被請示「要不要再點一盤」的人。

en.content:
Junior adult at family gatherings: watches the kids, runs to refill the soup, gets seated at the table in the far corner.

Senior adult at family gatherings: takes the head seat, decides whether to order more dishes, waves a hand at the bill and says 'I've got it.'

You've been in the workforce ten years, but at this round table, your seat is still the one closest to the bathroom.
en.editorNote: For the friend who still gets assigned to the kids' table at every family dinner — 30 years old and still waiting for the promotion
variations: Junior adult family-status indicator: you're still the one sent to refill the rice and the tea, not the one being asked 'should we order another plate?'

items.sourceNote: 'adulting is just Googling how to do things hoping nobody notices' viral line; rewritten original, distinct angle (faking confidence in front of others)

zh-tw.content:
成年人最厲害的技能，
不是真的什麼都會，
是在完全不會的時候，臉上還能保持「我懂、交給我」的表情。

然後轉身躲進廁所，瘋狂Google，再帶著自信走出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在會議上點頭如搗蒜、其實偷偷在桌下查資料的同事，懂的都懂
variations: 大人的自信有兩種：真的會，和Google完之後假裝自己一直都會。後者佔了百分之九十。

en.content:
An adult's most impressive skill
isn't actually knowing everything.

It's keeping a calm 'yeah, I've got this' face
while having absolutely no idea what's going on,

then slipping into the bathroom to frantically Google it
and walking back out radiating confidence.
en.editorNote: For the coworker nodding along in a meeting while secretly searching under the table — you know who you are
variations: Adult confidence comes in two flavors: actually knowing, and pretending you always knew right after you Googled it. The second one accounts for about 90 percent.

items.sourceNote: 'retirement plan = country with lower rent and more naps' viral money quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
問我有沒有退休規劃？

有啊。
計畫是搬到一個房租很便宜、午睡很合法、沒有人會在週一早上開會的地方。

至於那個地方在哪、要存多少錢才到得了——
這部分我還在研究，麻煩別問細節。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次聊到「退休金」就想躺平的朋友，苦中作樂剛剛好
variations: 我的理財目標很單純：賺到一個可以每天睡到自然醒、不用看老闆臉色的數字。目前進度：百分之三。

en.content:
You're asking if I have a retirement plan?

I do.
The plan is to move somewhere with cheap rent, legally mandated afternoon naps, and nobody scheduling meetings on Monday mornings.

Where that place is and how much I'd need to save to get there —
that part's still under research, so please don't ask for specifics.
en.editorNote: For the friend who wants to lie down every time 'retirement savings' comes up — the right amount of laughing through the pain
variations: My financial goal is simple: reach a number that lets me wake up naturally every day and never read my boss's mood again. Current progress: 3 percent.

items.sourceNote: 'exhausted at 9 PM, wide awake at 3 AM replaying awkward moments' viral adulting quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
當大人的睡眠時程：

晚上九點——眼皮重得像掛了兩袋米，撐不住。

凌晨三點——突然清醒，開始回放二〇一四年那句講錯的話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明很累卻每天半夜跟自己過不去的朋友，深夜限定共鳴
variations: 白天精神不濟，躺到床上瞬間變成偵探，把這十年所有尷尬瞬間重新偵辦一遍。

en.content:
An adult's sleep schedule:

9 PM — eyelids heavy as sandbags, can't keep them open.

3 AM — suddenly wide awake, rerunning that one thing I said wrong back in 2014.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's dead tired all day but picks a fight with their own memory at midnight
variations: Can't focus all day, then the second my head hits the pillow I turn into a detective reopening every cringe case from the last decade.

items.sourceNote: 'choosing which bill to panic over first' viral money quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
小時候以為長大就是想買什麼買什麼。

長大後才發現，所謂的財務自由，是每個月在一堆帳單裡，決定先為哪一張慌張。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個月初看到帳單就開始排序焦慮的朋友，笑中帶淚剛剛好
variations: 我的存款不是數字，是傳說。每個月都有人說它存在，但從來沒人親眼見過。

en.content:
As a kid I thought being grown up meant buying whatever I wanted.

Turns out 'financial freedom' just means deciding which bill to panic over first each month.
en.editorNote: For the friend who opens their statements at the start of the month and immediately starts ranking their anxieties
variations: My savings account isn't a number, it's a legend. Every month someone insists it exists, but nobody's ever actually seen it.

items.sourceNote: 'adulting is just Googling how to do things while hoping nobody notices' viral quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
所謂當大人，就是一邊偷偷估狗「這個到底怎麼用」，一邊裝出一副「我從小就會」的表情。

而且每個人都在演，只是沒人承認。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個開會時點頭如搗蒜、回家立刻查剛剛在點頭什麼的同事
variations: 成年人的自信，百分之八十來自瀏覽器無痕分頁裡的那些基礎問題。

en.content:
Being an adult is secretly Googling 'how does this even work' while wearing a face that says 'I've known this since birth.'

And everyone's doing it — nobody just admits it.
en.editorNote: For the coworker who nods confidently in meetings, then goes home to look up what they were nodding at
variations: Eighty percent of adult confidence comes from the basic questions hiding in an incognito tab.

items.sourceNote: 'money comes in quietly and leaves dramatically' viral money quote; rewritten original

zh-tw.content:
薪水進帳的時候，安安靜靜，連個通知都怕吵到我。

離開的時候，倒是浩浩蕩蕩，房租、卡費、訂閱費，排隊跟我揮手道別。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個發薪日當天就知道這筆錢不屬於自己的朋友
variations: 我跟錢的關係很像住宿同學：來去匆匆，相處時間極短，而且永遠是它先離開。

en.content:
When my paycheck arrives, it tiptoes in — quiet, like it's afraid to wake me.

When it leaves, it's a whole parade: rent, credit card, subscriptions, all lined up waving goodbye.
en.editorNote: For the friend who knows, the moment they get paid, that the money was never really theirs
variations: My relationship with money is like a dorm roommate: brief, fleeting, and they're always the one who moves out first.
